    {

      },
      "outputs": [],
      "source": [
        "import pandas as pd\n",
        "import numpy as np\n",
        "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
        "import seaborn as sns\n",
        "from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split\n",
        "from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression\n",
        "from sklearn import metrics\n",
        "\n",
        "# Reading the dataset\n",
        "customers = pd.read_csv('Ecommerce Customers (1)')"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Display the first few rows\n",
        "print(customers.head())\n",
        "\n",
        "# Check data types and missing values\n",
        "print(customers.info())\n",
        "\n",
        "# Statistical summary\n",
        "print(customers.describe())"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "Lb36J54QjQ2Z",

    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "Using head() to see the structure, info() to check for null values and data types, and describe() to understand the distribution of the numerical data."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "4E8-v9CDjZKt"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Checking for missing values\n",
        "print(customers.isnull().sum())"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "Nbw-rzbtjbPk",
        "outputId": "34df8c2c-11cb-45dc-8030-a584ccf2c7ba"
      },
      "execution_count": null,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "stream",
          "name": "stdout",
          "text": [
            "Email                   0\n",
            "Address                 0\n",
            "Avatar                  0\n",
            "Avg. Session Length     0\n",
            "Time on App             0\n",
            "Time on Website         0\n",
            "Length of Membership    0\n",
            "Yearly Amount Spent     0\n",
            "dtype: int64\n"
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "Verifying if there are any missing values that need to be handled before modeling. (The dataset is usually clean)."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "11NuUSFyjhaV"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# We will focus on numerical features and ignore text columns like Email and Address\n",
        "# Numerical features are: Avg. Session Length, Time on App, Time on Website, and Length of Membership"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "fTu2KGRnjjS2"
      },
      "execution_count": null,
      "outputs": []
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "Identifying the relevant numerical features that will be used as inputs for our predictive model."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "j04qRJtljuQB"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Define Features (X) and Target (y)\n",
        "X = customers[['Avg. Session Length', 'Time on App', 'Time on Website', 'Length of Membership']]\n",
        "y = customers['Yearly Amount Spent']\n",
        "\n",
        "# Split the data into Training and Testing sets\n",
        "X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X, y, test_size=0.3, random_state=101)"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "iIOlfrW0jxDv"
      },
      "execution_count": null,
      "outputs": []
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "Setting 'Yearly Amount Spent' as the target variable and splitting the data (70% for training and 30% for testing) to evaluate the model's performance on unseen data."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "X0p1bPqOj4Lr"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Initialize and train the Linear Regression model\n",
        "lm = LinearRegression()\n",
        "lm.fit(X_train, y_train)\n",
        "\n",
        "# Print the coefficients\n",
        "print('Coefficients: \\n', lm.coef_)"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "5XOTyghGj8gr",
        "outputId": "f0bdda68-c3ee-431d-a7a2-3128af5a4c45"
      },
      "execution_count": null,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "stream",
          "name": "stdout",
          "text": [
            "Coefficients: \n",
            " [25.98154972 38.59015875  0.19040528 61.27909654]\n"
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "Fitting the Linear Regression model to the training data and checking the coefficients to see how much each feature contributes to the total spent."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "HKTRzVi8kE_8"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Make predictions\n",
        "predictions = lm.predict(X_test)\n",
        "\n",
        "# Calculate evaluation metrics\n",
        "print('MAE:', metrics.mean_absolute_error(y_test, predictions))\n",
        "print('MSE:', metrics.mean_squared_error(y_test, predictions))\n",
        "print('RMSE:', np.sqrt(metrics.mean_squared_error(y_test, predictions)))\n",
        "print('R^2 Score:', metrics.r2_score(y_test, predictions))"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "6Sugf4-ekWgJ",
        "outputId": "0007b29f-554c-4910-84e9-8030ec5d1afb"
      },
      "execution_count": null,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "stream",
          "name": "stdout",
          "text": [
            "MAE: 7.228148653430826\n",
            "MSE: 79.81305165097427\n",
            "RMSE: 8.933815066978624\n",
            "R^2 Score: 0.9890046246741234\n"
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "Comparing the model’s predictions against the actual values using metrics like MAE, RMSE, and R-squared to determine how accurate the model is."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "uDrAaEO4kZ_S"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "import seaborn as sns\n",
        "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
        "%matplotlib inline\n",
        "\n",
        "sns.set_style('whitegrid')\n",
        "\n",
        "# Creating the jointplot\n",
        "sns.jointplot(x='Time on Website', y='Yearly Amount Spent', color='purple', data=customers)"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/",
          "height": 624
        },
        "id": "dk-YsVVTs78E",
        "outputId": "4354885f-1cde-4d91-a64a-507d9fa16d67"
      },
      "execution_count": null,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "execute_result",
          "data": {
            "text/plain": [
              "<seaborn.axisgrid.JointGrid at 0x7f3681d165a0>"
            ]
          },
          "metadata": {},
          "execution_count": 8
        },
        {
          "output_type": "display_data",
          "data": {
            "text/plain": [
              "<Figure size 600x600 with 3 Axes>"
            ],
            "image/png": "\n"
          },
          "metadata": {}
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "This jointplot visualizes the relationship between the time customers spend on the website and their yearly spending. In this dataset, we can observe a very weak correlation between these two variables, as the data points are widely scattered."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "hZ0alYoSt6Ry"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "sns.jointplot(x='Time on App', y='Yearly Amount Spent', color='orange', data=customers)"
      ],

    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "This figure shows the correlation between the time spent on the mobile application and yearly spending. Unlike the website plot, this one shows a clearer linear trend, indicating that as the time spent on the App increases, the total spending tends to increase as well."
      ],

    {

        "sns.pairplot(customers)"

      },
      "execution_count": null,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "execute_result",
          "data": {

              "<seaborn.axisgrid.PairGrid at 0x7f36807f8830>"
            ]
          },

        {

    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "The pairplot generates a matrix of scatter plots and histograms for all numerical features. Based on this visualization, \"Length of Membership\" shows the strongest linear correlation with \"Yearly Amount Spent\", which is a key insight for our regression model."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "lEBQLAa7uUSj"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "sns.lmplot(x='Length of Membership', y='Yearly Amount Spent', data=customers)"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "hxhTcWJNvHtb",
        "outputId": "25877d26-99e5-44fc-8acb-4fa27b51956a",
        "colab": {

        }
      },
      "execution_count": null,
      "outputs": [
        {

        {

    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "This lmplot displays a strong linear relationship between the duration of a customer's membership and their yearly spending. The narrow shaded area suggests a high level of confidence in the fit, justifying the use of a Linear Regression model."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "8t3aetpIusP6"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "**Conclusion**:\n",
        "The Linear Regression model performed exceptionally well with an R-squared score of 0.99, indicating a very strong fit. The analysis shows that \"Length of Membership\" is the most significant factor in predicting yearly spend, followed by \"Time on App.\" Based on these results, the company should focus on customer retention and enhancing the mobile app experience to maximize revenue."
      ],

      }
